frameworks.name: successor representation
frameworks.one_line: state encoding by predicted future states, learned probabilistically across contexts

frameworks.name: model-based vs model-free reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: two classical RL strategies between which SR provides an intermediate

frameworks.name: Bayesian uncertainty-weighted prediction-error learning
frameworks.one_line: updates balance new observations against existing knowledge given uncertainty

frameworks.name: hippocampal successor representation
frameworks.one_line: hippocampus hypothesized to encode SR for future-reward prediction

core_question: How can the successor representation be learned and arbitrated across contexts when uncertainty and task switches matter?
key_assumptions: agents encode states via predicted future state occupancy (SR)
learning should adapt under uncertainty in observations vs prior knowledge
multiple contexts require multiple task-specific SRs
context inference can be driven by prediction accuracy
manipulations: introduction of contextual switches requiring task-specific SRs
varying uncertainty between new observations and existing knowledge
tasks demanding contextual memory and generalisation
key_findings: probabilistic SR learning rule optimally weights new evidence against prior estimates
model maintains and arbitrates multiple task-specific SRs across contexts
context selection driven by predictive accuracy of each SR
model captures animal behaviour in contextual memory and generalisation tasks
interpretations: SR learning should be cast as Bayesian inference balancing uncertainty
multi-context SR arbitration accounts for behaviour beyond single-task SR
